architecture_fit.note: Summing unnormalized dot products is the right anti-saturation idea and tracks count monotonically, but it is bare NumPy (no attention layer / base_model delta) and quantitatively barely beats baseline.

baseline_comparison.note: Constant-predictor baseline is measured and charted, but the decisive softmax-saturation strawman the goal hinges on is never shown.

faithfulness.note: No ablation/causal check, and the README describes a dominant-direction projection mechanism that main.py never implements (it just sums dot products).

operating_range.note: Only counts 1-8 (<1 order of magnitude) and accuracy silently collapses to 0 at m>=6 (robustness 0.0), contradicting the README claim that it tracks the baseline.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Mechanism is fully hand-built with no training and reproduces a monotone count signal, but calibration is poor so it barely clears the baseline.

visual_judgement.note: app.py fails the boot-check with an IndentationError at line 58, so the Demo tab cannot launch.

visualisation_rationale.note: Per-slice MAE-vs-baseline chart is a defensible choice but omits the headline accuracy and softmax strawman, and the rationale falsely claims the model does not degenerate at high counts.

notes: The unnormalized-dot-product sum produces a strongly monotone count signal (correlation ~1.0) but is uncalibrated: it systematically underestimates, so exact accuracy (0.132) barely clears the constant baseline (0.125) and robustness is 0.0 as accuracy collapses to zero at high counts. Faithfulness is undermined by a README that describes a different mechanism than main.py actually runs, with no ablation. The Gradio app does not even import (IndentationError), so the visualisation is unusable.
